official.name: Jeff Landry
official.office_address: Office of Governor, Baton Rouge, LA

summary.narrative: Landry took office Jan 2024 with explicit agenda on law enforcement, education, taxes, economic growth. Moved quickly via special sessions to pass conservative legislation on school vouchers, criminal justice, tax reform, Ten Commandments posting. Self-described pragmatic MAGA approach; pursued high-profile national conservative positions (nitrogen execution, Gulf of America naming).
summary.grade_rationale: Strong execution on legislative agenda within first 2 years; delivered on core promises (vouchers, tax reform, law enforcement, education mandates). Economic ranking improved 31st→18th. Some promises lack measurable outcomes or remain partial. National controversy suggests mixed stakeholder results.

promises.normalized: Expand school choice and voucher program
Reform tax system, reduce income tax reliance
Mandate Ten Commandments posting in public schools

data_gaps: Specific metrics on job creation outcomes vs. baseline
Quantified results from healthcare cost/access initiatives
Election security implementation details and audit results
Parent rights in education—specific legislation passed
School voucher uptake rates and fiscal impact
Income tax reduction amounts and revenue replacement sources
Details on coach compensation metrics for LSU football
Execution methodology implementation timeline and challenges
